comment:
These should be two different excerpts: "الطلاق: في اللغة: حل الوثاق. مشتق من الإطلاق، وهو الترك والإرسال." is one, because it is the "تعريف الطلاق لغة". Then, "وفي الشرع: حَل عقدة التزويج، والتعريف الشرعي فَرْد من معناه اللغوي العام. قال إمام. الحرمين: هو لفظ جاهلي ورد الشرع بتقريره." is an excerpt (تعريف الطلاق شرعا), BUT importantly:

1 - this excerpt starts with a "cut-off-bit": the owner reading "وفي الشرع ..." is left with questions; this clearly builds on the previous part (which is تعريف الطلاق لغة), which in this case is important context;. Excerpts should be self contained, which includes answering all questions and confusions that may arise during the owner's usage of the library.

2 - The second excerpt, "والتعريف الشرعي فَرْد من معناه اللغوي العام. قال إمام. الحرمين: هو لفظ جاهلي ورد الشرع بتقريره." should NOT be its own excerpt. This is too granual and has diminished results. Technically, "وفي الشرع: حَل عقدة التزوي" answers the question: "تعريف الطلاق شرعا" while "والتعريف الشرعي فَرْد من معناه اللغوي العام. قال إمام. الحرمين: هو لفظ جاهلي ورد الشرع بتقريره." answers the question: "how are these two definitions related"? But this is too granual and would cause more harm than good. 

The principle for this is: the author could have written "والتعريف الشرعي فَرْد من معناه اللغوي العام. قال إمام. الحرمين: هو لفظ جاهلي ورد الشرع بتقريره." as a seperate sentence (instead of after a comma), and it would make sense. But this sentence is closely related to the previous one ("وفي الشرع: حَل عقدة التزوي"), in a way that when I"m reading "وفي الشرع: حَل عقدة التزوي" and I immediately read "والتعريف الشرعي فَرْد من معناه اللغوي العام. قال إمام. الحرمين: هو لفظ جاهلي ورد الشرع بتقريره." within the same one self contained excerpt, I would get a deeper understanding than if I would have to read two different excerpts. I should not read an excerpt containing "وفي الشرع: حَل عقدة التزوي", think I'm ready to move on, then later come across the excerpt containing "والتعريف الشرعي فَرْد من معناه اللغوي العام. قال إمام. الحرمين: هو لفظ جاهلي ورد الشرع بتقريره." and ave to re-evaluate my understanding!

So: excerpting should definitely not blindly be "this is one self contained unit, so it's an excerpt"; no, there are many hints that hint at what atoms should form excerpts; continous sentence, too closely related, oppertunistic grouping (like in this case; me reading both in one excerpt gives way more oppertunity for deep understanding than seperate).

This also reminds me of an important principle I believe is new: the relationship between excerpts. Some excerpts - even though they speak about different topics - still need to be connected in some way (not talking about content, but about outer connection). To clarify:

Imagine I'm reading "وفي الشرع: حَل عقدة التزويج، والتعريف الشرعي فَرْد من معناه اللغوي العام. قال إمام. الحرمين: هو لفظ جاهلي ورد الشرع بتقريره.". The way this excerpt is to be understood depends on identifying the right other excerpt from "تعريف الطلاق لغة", which in this case would be the excerpt containing "الطلاق: في اللغة: حل الوثاق. مشتق من الإطلاق، وهو الترك والإرسال.". If I read the second excerpt, then realize I want to review the first excerpt, then open the leaf in the taxonomy tree and open another "تعريف الطلاق لغة" from another source, then that is a CORRUPTION in my knowledge: I TRY TO UNDERSTAND ONE EXCERPT BASED ON ANOTHER MISMATCHED EXCERPT!


Final note: تعريف الطلاق لغة & تعريف الطلاق شرعا should be different leafs in the taxonomy tree (which is part of the taxonomy engine). But if it wasn't, then that would justify this excerpt being only one excerpt; it would then be entirely placed under the leaf "تعريف الطلاق". However, that is not what this library is aiming for. It should be wisely and intelligently granulated without absurd granuality nor too broad.

excerpt_context.text_snippet:
‌‌كتاب الطلاق
الطلاق: في اللغة: حل الوثاق. مشتق من الإطلاق، وهو الترك والإرسال.

comment:
This is still too broad ... Imagine we keep this broad framework for thousands of excerpts. The topic "مشروعية الطلاق" would have thousands of excerpts, each excerpt mixes: stating the rule in general, stating the proofs in general, static the specific proofs from the quran, static the specific proofs from the sunnah ... That goes against the point of excerpting ... I might aswell just take picture of books I read, because that is what this is esentially doing; just cutting up half/entire pages from books and calling them excerpts ... this is not the work the excerpting engine was meant to facilitate. It was meant to failitate what is humanly very hard / impossible, as to give me the edge over any previous scholars.

 It should be like this:

EXCERPT1: [وحكمه ثابت في الكتاب، والسنة، والإجماع، والقياس الصحيح]. Reason: it is a general stating of the rule. Imagine a leaf like this:

|____الحكم إجمالا
            |____ EXCERPT FROM SOURCE 1
            |____ EXCERPT FROM SOURCE 2
            |____ EXCERPT FROM SOURCE 3
            |____ EXCERPT FROM SOURCE ...

Do you see the edge this would give me? I open the granulated leaf and get exactly what I want: a direct overview of what all that was ever said on the general ruling. I can directly compare the opinion (not yet the proofs) of every scholar that has ever spoken about this topic.

EXCERPT2: [فأما الكتاب فنحو {الطلاقُ مَرتَانِ} وعيرها من الآيات.] . This will give me the edge; seeing how the different scholars did their proofing from the quran. 
|____الاستدلال
            |____ من الكتاب
                       |____ EXCERPT FROM SOURCE 2
                       |____ EXCERPT FROM SOURCE 3
                       |____ EXCERPT FROM SOURCE ...

Even better: X من الكتاب > آية ; per ayah. So the granuality should be: proofing, from quran, from this ayah; that is how the excerpting should be done. If there were multiple quranic verses in this excerpts, they should have produced multiple excerpt; one per quranic verse.

That is the first principle. The second and equally important to get right principle is preserving the context. I do not want to open the leaf that has all proofings from scholars concerning a specific ayah, then have to solve a puzzle: this excerpt belongs to this other excerpt ... trying to solve puzzle of what opinion a proofing is trying to justify. This is extremely important to get right. 

So two very important aspects, which come back to what I had documented at the very beginning of the excerpting engine;

1 - the excerpting itself; how should it be done?
2 - the metadata and context preservation

Additionally:

3 - self containment and leaving the owner with no questionmarks.

NOTE:

I'm starting to realize: maybe we are losing excerpting potential because the taxonomy trees are not available yet ... I'm starting to think: is it better to let the excerpting be done without providing any information about the taxonomy we have (for unbiased excerpting), or would it be better to deliver the taxonomy tree with? The second worries me, bacause it may lead to excerpting being done based on the tree, not unbiased (so that the tree can objectively be evolved).

excerpt_context.text_snippet:
وحكمه ثابت في الكتاب، والسنة، والإجماع، والقياس الصحيح.
فأما الكتاب فنحو {الطلاقُ
